meta_title: For Founders: client workflow to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Client Workflow to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Stop drowning in tutorial hell. Learn a professional client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your SaaS MVP faster than ever before.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop consuming and start shipping. By adopting a 'client-first' workflow, you treat code as a means to a business outcome, not an end. Using Antigravity to scaffold logic and Claude Code to execute iterations, you move from passive watching to active revenue-generating development.
content.intro:
The screen glows with the fifth video of a 'Full-Stack SaaS' playlist, but your repository remains empty. You are trapped in the illusion of progress, mistaking tutorial consumption for actual construction. It is the silent killer of startups: the comfort of the guided path, where every error is solved for you before it even manifests.

Real founders don't follow tutorials; they solve problems. To break this cycle, you must shift your mindset from 'learning to code' to 'building to sell.' This workflow treats your IDE like a business engine, utilizing advanced AI tooling to bridge the gap between abstract requirements and production-ready features.

steps.title: Define the Business Primitive
steps.description: Before writing a single line, define the core value proposition. If you can't describe it in one sentence, you aren't ready to code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Generate a lean database schema and API structure for a SaaS product that solves [INSERT PROBLEM]. Prioritize speed to market and scalability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a project README.md outlining the MVP core features and user flow'.

steps.title: Scaffold the Core Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate. Don't waste time on configuration; focus on the business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular Next.js architecture with Supabase integration. Include authentication, a billing subscription flow, and a protected dashboard route.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project structure and install dependencies based on the generated architecture'.

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Build one feature at a time. Do not move forward until the feature is testable and functional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the server-side logic for a subscription-based data export feature. Ensure it handles edge cases like failed payments and rate limiting.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the data export module and add unit tests for the subscription check logic'.

steps.title: Business Logic Validation
steps.description: Simulate real-world user scenarios to ensure the product solves the problem identified in Step 1.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of integration tests that simulate a user signing up, subscribing, and performing the core product action.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run test suite and fix any regressions in the authentication or billing flow'.

steps.title: Production Deployment Setup
steps.description: Automate your deployment pipeline so you can ship updates daily without manual friction.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for automated testing and deployment to Vercel. Include environment variable management.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Configure CI/CD pipeline and verify production build readiness'.

pro_tips: Treat every bug as a feature request; if you spend more than 30 minutes debugging, step back and rethink the architectural approach.
Ship the 'ugly' version first; your users care about the solution, not the elegance of your backend code.
Use AI to write your documentation as you code; it forces you to understand the logic you just generated.
Focus on 'Time to First Dollar'—prioritize the payment flow above all other features.
Never hard-code configuration; keep your environment variables strictly isolated from your source code.
content.next_steps: Now that your architecture is live, focus on acquiring your first 10 beta users. Do not add new features until you have validated the core loop with actual feedback.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the workflow, and the mindset to stop watching and start winning. The only thing missing is your execution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
